description_long: This is a vintage, boxy filing cabinet with a dark brown exterior and a robust, rectangular form. The front features a single large door with a rounded knob and subtle vertical ridges at the bottom, giving it an Art Deco appearance. When opened, the interior reveals a mechanical system with spools and levers, suggesting it may be a specialized archival or microfilm storage cabinet. The sides are plain and flat, while the back is solid and unadorned. The cabinet sits on a slightly raised base, providing stability and a slight elevation from the ground. The overall construction appears to be primarily metal, with some plastic or Bakelite components for the knobs and interior mechanisms.
description: A dark brown, vintage filing cabinet with a single front door, rounded knob, and visible internal mechanical components. The design is robust and boxy, with subtle Art Deco detailing.
description_view_0: Side view showing the solid, flat panel and the protruding front door with a rounded knob.
description_view_1: Front view with the door open, revealing internal spools and mechanical parts, indicating specialized storage.
description_view_2: Opposite side view, similar to view 0, showing the plain side and the edge of the door.
description_view_3: Rear view displaying a solid, unadorned back panel and the raised base.
